title: 1.1: Tests that resemblesUrl() returns true when given a fully-formed URL with protocol prefix
1.2: Tests that resemblesUrl() returns true when given a URL containing a query string
1.3: Tests that resemblesUrl() returns true for URLs with a two-character TLD
1.4: Tests that resemblesUrl() returns false for inputs with adjacent dots
1.5: Tests that resemblesUrl() returns false for inputs with spaced dots
1.6: Tests that resemblesUrl() returns false for a single dot input
1.7: Tests that resemblesUrl() returns false for plain strings without dots
1.8: Tests that resemblesUrl() returns true when given a domain-only URL without an explicit protocol scheme.
2.1: Places the test for protocol-prefixed URL detection in client/lib/url/test/resembles-url.js.
2.2: Places the test for domain-only URL detection without protocol in client/lib/url/test/resembles-url.js.
2.3: Places the test for URL detection with query string parameters in client/lib/url/test/resembles-url.js.
2.4: Places the test for URL detection with short two-character TLD suffixes in client/lib/url/test/resembles-url.js.
2.5: Places the test for rejecting malformed inputs with consecutive dots in client/lib/url/test/resembles-url.js.
2.6: Places the test for rejecting inputs with dots separated by spaces in client/lib/url/test/resembles-url.js.
2.7: Places the test for rejecting a single dot character input in client/lib/url/test/resembles-url.js.
2.8: Places the test for rejecting plain strings without any dot separator in client/lib/url/test/resembles-url.js.
3.1: All tests use test() instead of it()
3.2: All tests declare input using a named const variable
3.3: All tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert structure
4.1: Follows a consistent pattern for positive URL detection cases where valid inputs assert resemblesUrl(source) is true.
